name: Saffron Rice, Spicy Black Beans, Chunky Salsa
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dried black beans, soaked overnight and drained
1 Chili (jalapeno or serrano), seeded and coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the beans: In a 3-quart saucepan, combine the beans, water, onion, carrot, green peppers, chili, garlic, bay leaf, soy sauce, cumin, and red pepper flakes. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally. Reduce the heat to medium and simmer, uncovered, for about 2-1/2 hours, or until the beans are tender and almost all the liquid has been absorbed. Discard the bay leaf.
2. To make the rice: In a 1-quart saucepan, bring 1/4 of the stock to a boil. Add the saffron. Cover, remove from heat, and let stand for 5 minutes.
3. In a 2-quart saucepan over low heat, sauté onions in oil until tender (about 5 minutes). Add garlic and sauté for 1 minute. Stir in turmeric, then the rice. Add remaining stock and saffron mixture. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the rice is tender and all the liquid has been absorbed.
4. Lightly coat 4 (8-ounce) custard cups with no-stick spray or olive oil. Spoon about 3/4 cup of warm rice into each cup, pressing it into the bottom and up the sides. Add about 1/2 cup of beans to each cup. Top with more rice to cover the beans; lightly pack it around the edges.
5. If not serving immediately, cover each cup tightly with a piece of foil. Set cups on a baking sheet and keep warm at 300°F (for up to 30 minutes).
6. To make the salsa: In a medium bowl, combine diced tomatoes, avocado, onions, cucumbers, coriander, lime juice, oil, and peppers.
7. Serve by removing foil from each cup, inverting onto dinner plates, topping with salsa and soy yogurt, and garnishing with coriander. Serves 4.
